blocks.title: 📘 CONDICIONALES · 1st & 2nd
blocks.intro: Para negociar necesitas condicionales: el 1st conditional para cosas posibles y reales, y el 2nd conditional para situaciones hipotéticas. Repasa y luego practica.

cells.body:
If + present, will + verbo
If you confirm today, we will start Monday. (posible / real)

If + past, would + verbo
If we had more time, we would add more features. (hipotético)

unless = if not:
We can't start unless you confirm. (a menos que)

as long as = siempre que:
We'll finish on time as long as nothing changes.

If you will confirm…
If you confirm… (no 'will' después de if)
If I were you, I'd…

blocks.ladderLabel: Escala de negociación — frases con condicionales, de más directa a más diplomática:
ladder: If you don't pay, we stop.
Unless you confirm, we can't start.
If you confirm today, we'll start Monday.
As long as the scope stays, we're fine.
If we had more budget, we'd do it all.
blocks.note: Cómo funciona: esta lección mezcla completar 📝, elegir respuestas 💬, escucha 🎧 y unir 🔗. Haz el ✓ CHECK en cada ejercicio.

blocks.title: ★ THE SCOPE NEGOTIATION
blocks.intro: David quiere más funciones para GreenMarket… pero Priya controla el presupuesto y no cede fácil. Marcos queda en el medio: tiene que negociar el alcance con condicionales — ofertas, condiciones y consecuencias.

blocks.title: COMPLETA EL CONDICIONAL
blocks.intro: Elige la forma correcta. Piensa: ¿posible y real (1st) o hipotético (2nd)?

items.q: If you confirm today, we ___ on Monday.
If we ___ more time, we would add a mobile app.
We can't begin ___ you sign the agreement.
If the budget ___ bigger, we'd hire another developer.
We'll deliver on time ___ the scope doesn't change.
If you ___ the extra feature, there will be an extra cost.

blocks.title: ELIGE TU OFERTA
blocks.intro: Estás negociando con un cliente. Elige la respuesta con el condicional correcto — y el tono profesional.

lines.html: We need the app AND the website for the same price.

options: If you increase the budget a little, we'll build both.
If you will pay more, we build both.

lines.html: And what would you do with a bigger budget, hypothetically?

options: If we had a bigger budget, we would add a mobile app too.
If we have a bigger budget, we will added apps.
I am from the web team.

lines.q: Pones una condición clara pero amable:
options: Yes — as long as the scope doesn't change, we'll deliver on time.
Unless we deliver, the deadline is Friday.

lines.html: And if I don't confirm this week?

options: Unless you confirm this week, we can't start until next month.
If you don't confirm, is your problem.

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · THE NEGOTIATION CALL
blocks.desc: David pide más funciones. Priya defiende el presupuesto. Marcos busca el acuerdo. ¿Quién consigue qué?

questions.q: What does David want to add to the project?
What is Priya's condition for building the app?
What do they agree in the end?
options: The app will be phase two, with a discount

questions.q: Marcos promises to deliver the website on time as long as the scope doesn't change.

speaking.prompt: Summarize the deal. Use if, unless and as long as.
speaking.starter: If David confirms this week, the team will… The app will be phase two, and as long as…

transcript.html: Thanks for joining, both. David, you wanted to talk about the scope?
Yes. The website looks great, but we also want a mobile app. If we launch without an app, we 'll lose younger customers.
I understand, David, but the app isn't in the budget. If we had a bigger budget, we would build it — happily. Right now, we can't.
Hmm. What would it cost, hypothetically?
Roughly thirty percent more. And unless we start planning it early, it won't be ready this year.
Can I suggest something? If we make the app phase two, we 'll finish the website on time — and David, if you confirm phase two this week, we 'll give you a ten percent discount on it.
That sounds fair. And the website deadline?
As long as the scope doesn't change again, we'll deliver on the fifteenth. Deal?
Deal. I'll confirm phase two on Friday.

blocks.title: UNE LA CONDICIÓN CON SU RESULTADO
blocks.intro: Toca la cláusula con if y luego su resultado lógico.

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · THE DEBRIEF
blocks.desc: Después de la llamada, Marcos y Sofia repasan el acuerdo y planean la fase dos.

questions.q: What discount did Marcos offer David?
What will Sofia do if David confirms on Friday?
Sofia thinks the deal was a bad idea.

speaking.prompt: What would YOU do if a client asked for more features without more budget? Use 1–2 conditionals.
speaking.starter: If a client asked for more features, I would… Unless they increased the budget, I…

transcript.html: Marcos! I heard the call went well. So, what's the deal?
Pretty good. If David confirms this week, we 'll build the app as phase two — with a ten percent discount.
Yes. She said if we had a bigger budget, she would build it now — but phase two works for everyone.
Great. If he confirms on Friday, I 'll start planning phase two next week. And the website?
On track. As long as nothing changes, we deliver on the fifteenth. Unless the server crashes again, of course!
Don't even joke about that! Nice work, Marcos. You're getting good at this.

blocks.title: ✍️ THE COUNTER-OFFER EMAIL
blocks.intro: Un cliente pide más funciones sin más presupuesto. Escribe tu contraoferta (5–7 frases):
checklist: Agradece el interés y resume el pedido
Una condición real con 1st conditional (If you…, we will…)
Una hipótesis con 2nd conditional (If we had…, we would…)
Usa unless o as long as una vez
Cierra pidiendo confirmación (Could you confirm by…?)
blocks.placeholder: Hi David, Thanks for your message about…
blocks.example: Hi (name), Thanks for your message about (topic) — it's a great idea. If you (condition), we'll (result). If we had (imaginary thing), we would (action), but (reality). As long as (condition), we'll (promise). Could you confirm by (day)? Best, (your name)
blocks.hw: Escribe tu email de contraoferta y envíamelo por WhatsApp para corregirlo juntos.
